description: Figure 3 illustrates the evolution of retained austenite (RA) during thermomechanical processing of a low-density Fe-0.25C-3Mn-2Al-0.4Si steel. Panels (a)-(d) display EBSD phase maps where the BCC phase (ferrite/martensite) appears red and the FCC phase (austenite) appears green. The hot-rolled sample HR (a) shows 100% BCC phase with zero RA fraction. After intercritical annealing at 750°C, sample AN750 (b) exhibits 3.0 vol% RA with an average diameter of 0.59 μm. As annealing temperature increases to 800°C (AN800, c) and 850°C (AN850, d), RA content decreases to 1.1 vol% and 0.2 vol% respectively, while the average diameter increases to 0.72 μm and 0.83 μm. Panel (e) presents a line chart summarizing this quantitative trend, confirming that RA content peaks at 750°C. Panel (f) shows XRD patterns verifying the coexistence of BCC (α) and FCC (γ) phases, with the γ peaks progressively diminishing at higher annealing temperatures. These results demonstrate that annealing at 750°C optimally stabilizes RA, which is essential for activating the TRIP effect and enhancing mechanical properties.
